steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to explain why Cree is classified as a synthetic language. A good answer must name the defining feature of synthetic languages and show that Cree has it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Cree is a synthetic language rather than an analytic language, where nouns rely on word order and auxiliary words to indicate their function.': Describes what analytic languages do, not why Cree is synthetic. ✗
  • Choice B — 'In contrast to an analytic language, a synthetic language like Cree indicates a noun"s function within a sentence.': Vague — all languages indicate noun function somehow; the how is never named. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Cree has a robust case system in which nouns take different forms depending on their function, making it a synthetic language.': Names the key feature (case system, nouns changing form by function) and explicitly links it to Cree"s classification as synthetic. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Cree is classified as a synthetic language, meaning that the Algonquian language utilizes nouns to fulfill various functions within a sentence.': Describes noun roles without identifying the mechanism (case changes). ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
